Based on the retrieved evidence, several studies confirm that Vitamin C significantly improves the absorption of non-heme iron from plant-based sources. These studies highlight how ascorbic acid facilitates both dissolution of ferric iron into ferrous form and reduces intestinal pH levels necessary for optimal iron uptake. In contrast, there is limited scientific support indicating similar benefits for the other vitamins mentioned regarding enhancing iron absorption specifically. Given these findings align closely with current medical consensus favoring Vitamin C’s unique role here,

In contrast, there is limited scientific support indicating similar benefits for the other vitamins mentioned regarding enhancing iron absorption specifically.
